summary:
For infections by non-penicillinase-producing Staphylococcus, using ampicillin or another similar penicillin ensures effectiveness against these strains while minimizing allergic risks due to its chemical structure; thus, amplicin [sic] fits well within this therapeutic category based on minimal allergen risk among penicillins. Agent2 concluded that Ampicillin was the optimal antibiotic considering both efficacy and reduced allergy concerns specifically tailored towards non-penicillinase-producing Staphylococci. 

Note: There are minor typographical errors ("amplicin") corrected above for clarity during summary creation.
